name: Neighborhood Housing Services Of South Florida
description_short: Non-profit housing counselor providing HUD-certified homebuyer education, first-time homeowner loans, and housing crisis assistance across South Florida.
description_long:
Neighborhood Housing Services of South Florida (NHSSF) is a non-profit organization serving Miami-Dade and Broward counties with a focus on expanding homeownership and providing housing stability. Founded to address barriers to homeownership, NHSSF has created 248 first-time homeowners over the last three years while providing thousands of counseling sessions and educational workshops to prospective buyers and current homeowners facing financial hardship.

NHSSF offers comprehensive housing services including HUD-certified homebuyer education classes (both in-person and online through eHome America), one-on-one financial counseling, first-time homebuyer loans, and specialized crisis assistance through their Housing Stability & Crisis Prevention (HSCP) program for those facing eviction or foreclosure. They provide flexible learning options with 8-hour workshops available in English and Spanish, as well as self-paced online alternatives. The organization also operates community programs including weatherization assistance and beautification initiatives.

The organization distinguishes itself through documented, measurable outcomes: 4,295 one-on-one counseling sessions, 35 first-time homebuyer loans, and 2,184 class attendees over three years. They maintain accessible walk-in hours at their Miami-Dade office (Monday-Friday 8:30 AM-5 PM) and offer appointment-based services in Broward. Client testimonials consistently highlight professional staff knowledge, comprehensive guidance, and tangible results in securing mortgages and achieving homeownership.

As a free-help non-profit, NHSSF is best suited for first-time homebuyers, renters seeking financial literacy, and homeowners in housing distress. The primary caveat is geographic limitation to South Florida (Miami-Dade and Broward counties) and that while homebuyer loan products are offered, their primary value lies in education and counseling rather than alternative lending. Prospective borrowers should confirm current loan product availability and terms.
pros: HUD-certified homebuyer education with documented track record of 248 first-time homeowners created in last 3 years
Flexible learning options including online self-paced eHome America classes to accommodate working schedules
Bilingual services with classes offered in English and Spanish
Crisis housing counseling specifically for eviction prevention and foreclosure assistance through HSCP program
Accessible walk-in hours at Miami-Dade location (8:30 AM-5 PM weekdays) for document drop-off
One-on-one personalized counseling with 4,295+ sessions provided in last 3 years
Free account creation and initial services as a non-profit organization
cons: Geographic service limitation to Miami-Dade and Broward counties only; not available statewide or nationally
Broward office operates by appointment only, limiting walk-in accessibility compared to Miami-Dade location
Limited information on website regarding specific loan terms, rates, amounts, or approval criteria for first-time homebuyer loans
No indication of Spanish-language availability for one-on-one counseling sessions, only classes
Upcoming class schedule shown ends in May 2026 with no visibility into regular ongoing class offerings
best_for: First-time homebuyers in South Florida seeking HUD-certified education and mortgage guidance
Renters facing eviction or foreclosure looking for crisis prevention counseling
Spanish-speaking prospective homeowners wanting bilingual financial literacy and homebuyer education
Borrowers unable to attend scheduled in-person classes who prefer self-paced online learning
diagnosis: NHSSF is best for first-time homebuyers and renters in Miami-Dade or Broward counties seeking free, nonprofit housing counseling, financial education, and crisis assistance. The main caveat is strict geographic limitation to South Florida and that while they offer first-time homebuyer loans, detailed lending terms and product specifics are not published on their website—direct contact required for borrowing details.
services: HUD-certified homebuyer education workshops (8-hour in-person classes)
Online self-paced homebuyer education through eHome America platform
One-on-one financial and housing counseling sessions
First-time homebuyer loan products and lending services
Housing Stability & Crisis Prevention (HSCP) program for eviction/foreclosure assistance
Homeownership post-purchase guidance and financial management counseling
Weatherization assistance program for energy efficiency
Online client portal for account management
Document drop-off services at Miami-Dade walk-in location
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
